personas.name: Global User Alex
personas.description: A non-native English speaker who needs the app's terminology accurately translated to log moods correctly.
personas.notes: The AI made Alex a generic 'UX seeker'. I updated his profile to reflect the real issue: he needs the app in his native language.

personas.name: Clinical Patient Mia
personas.description: A user in active therapy who uses the app to accurately report symptom patterns to her doctor.
pain_points: Forgetting specific incidents between therapy sessions
personas.notes: The AI completely hallucinated a 'supportive community' feature. I deleted that and grounded Mia's goals in sharing data with her doctor, which is what the reviews actually say.

personas.name: Privacy-Conscious Jake
personas.description: A skeptical user who demands total control over device data and clear value before paying for a subscription.
personas.notes: Adjusted Jake's pain points to specifically target the third-party device ID sharing mentioned in the reviews.

personas.name: Budget-Restricted Emily
personas.description: A user who wants professional-grade tracking but cannot afford expensive weekly therapy upsells.
pain_points: Sixty-five dollar per week therapy recommendations are unaffordable
personas.notes: Emily isn't looking for 'unique features' like the AI thought; she's just on a tight budget. Fixed her persona to reflect that.

personas.name: Crisis Tracker Sarah
personas.description: A user dealing with severe mental health episodes who needs the app to respond safely to extreme mood inputs.
pain_points: Dismissive automated responses to severe inputs
personas.notes: Made Sarah's persona focus on the crisis response features (specifically the 'suicidal thought' review) since the AI missed the urgency.
